title: Digital Signatures with Ed25519 and ECDSA · Contract Triage
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible versioned signed artifact verifier with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned signed artifact verifier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.
Generate, parse, sign, and verify Ed25519 keys and signatures with exact message/context rules and trusted key selection.
Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Signature Guarantees
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 5961 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.

Inspect the Crypto Go Signature Guarantees trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Signature Guarantees.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent. For Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Signature Guarantees, keep Crypto Go Signature Guarantees connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.
